zh.lifeSummary: Ursula Greygory於大約1183年出生在英國越可爾。她於大約1199年與William De Hywis在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Ursula Greygory于大约1183年出生在英国越可爾。她于大约1199年与William De Hywis在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Greygory
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from a personal name that was popular throughout Christendom in the Middle Ages. The Greek original, Grēgorios, is a derivative of grēgorein ‘to be awake, to be watchful’. However, the Latin form, Gregorius, came to be associated by folk etymology with grex, gregis ‘flock, herd’, under the influence of the Christian image of the good shepherd. The Greek name was borne in the early Christian centuries by two fathers of the Orthodox Church, Saint Gregory Nazianzene (c. 325–390) and Saint Gregory of Nyssa (c. 331–395), and later by sixteen popes, starting with Gregory the Great (c. 540–604). It was also the name of 3rd- and 4th-century apostles of Armenia. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Italian Gregorio , German, Slovak, and Slovenian Gregor , Polish Grzegorz, Czech Řehoř (see Rehor ), and French Gregoire , and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Polish Grzegorczyk .

variants.name: Goeres
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Markus.
North German (Göres): from a shortened form of the Latin personal name Gregorius (see Gregory , and compare Gorr ).

variants.name: Goers
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Klaus, Otto.
North German (Görs): patronymic from Goer, a Low German form of the Latin personal name Gregorius (see Gregory ).

variants.name: Gareis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Hans, Helmut.
German:
from a shortened form of the Latin personal name Gregorius (see Gregory ).

variants.name: Gohr
meanings: German: topographic name for someone living near a bog, from Middle Low German gor ‘quagmire, bog’, or a habitational name from any of the places called with this word, such as Gohr near Neuss.

East German (of Slavic origin): topographic name from Polish or other Slavic gora ‘mountain’ or from the root gor- (ultimately from Old Slavic gorěti) ‘to burn’ (denoting cleared forest), elements contained in various field names and placenames.
Germanized form of Sorbian Gór: from a Lower Sorbian short form of the personal name Gorisław (from an Old Slavic name based on gorěti ‘to burn’) or Grogor (see Gregory ).

variants.name: Gregorio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Mario, Fernando, Jose, Ramon, Juan, Roberto, Ana, Cesar, Francisco, Adolfo, Alberto.  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Salvatore, Carmine, Gennaro, Federico, Franco, Heriberto, Italo, Marco, Nicola.  Portuguese Henrique, Joaquim, Paulo.
Italian and Spanish; Portuguese (Gregório): from the personal name Gregorio, Gregório (see Gregory ). Compare Di Gregorio .

variants.name: Gergely
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Arpad, Bela, Attila, Dezso, Laszlo, Mihaly.
Hungarian: from the personal name Gergely, Hungarian form Gregory .

variants.name: Goris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Julio, Alicia, Manuel, Maribel, Rafael, Agusto, Ana, Bernardina, Edmundo, Eladio.
Galician (Gorís): habitational name from Gorís in Pontevedra province, Galicia (Spain).
Dutch: patronymic from a vernacular of the personal name Gregorius (see Gregory ).

variants.name: Gorr
meanings: German:
from a short form of the personal name Gregor, Latin Gregorius (see Gregory ).
nickname from Middle High German gorre ‘bad horse’.

variants.name: Gore
meanings: English: from Middle English gor(e), gar(e) ‘triangular piece of land’ (Old English gāra, a derivative of gār ‘spear’, with reference to the triangular shape of a spearhead), a topographic name for someone living by a triangular field, or a habitational name from any of various places, for example Gore Court in Tunstall (Kent) and Gore Farm in Hannington (Wiltshire), named from this word.

French: from Old French gore ‘sow’ (a word of allegedly imitative origin, reflecting the grunting of the animal), applied as a metonymic occupational name for a swineherd or as an unflattering nickname.
French: probably also from a pet form of a vernacular form of the personal name Grégoire (see Gregory ).

variants.name: Gori
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Gino, Italo, Lido, Marco, Aldo, Angelo, Dino, Domenic, Fabiano, Livio, Luca, Secondo.
Italian (northern): patronymic or plural form of the personal name Goro, a shortened form of Gregorio (see Gregory ).
Swiss German: from a pet form of the personal name Georg (see George ).

nameMeanings.name: Ursula
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin name Ursula, a diminutive of ursa ‘(she-)bear’. This was the name of a 4th-century saint martyred at Cologne with a number of companions, traditionally said to have been eleven thousand, but more probably just eleven, the exaggeration being due to a misreading of a diacritic mark in an early manuscript. This name was moderately popular in the 16th century, but its use in the English-speaking world today is selective. A more recent influence has been the film actress Ursula Andress ( b. 1936 in Switzerland).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: Ursula Greygory was born about 1183, in Yorkshire, England. She married William De Hywis about 1199, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Ursula Greygory est née environ 1183, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Elle a épousé William De Hywis environ 1199, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Ursula Greygory wurde um 1183 in Yorkshire, England geboren. Sie hat um 1199 in Yorkshire, England William De Hywis geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Ursula Greygory è nata il circa 1183 nel Yorkshire, England. Lei sposato William De Hywis circa 1199, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Ursula Greygoryは約1183年にイギリスヨークシュアイアで生まれた。約1199年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでWilliam De Hywisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Ursula Greygory(은)는 대략 1183년 영국 요르크실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1199년 영국 요르크실르에서 William De Hywis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Ursula Greygory nasceu aproximadamente 1183, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com William De Hywis aproximadamente 1199, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Ursula Greygory родилась около 1183 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William De Hywis около 1199 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Ursula Greygory nació aproximadamente 1183, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con William De Hywis aproximadamente 1199, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
